rules.check: Health claims are prohibited unless authorised and listed on the EU Register, even if scientifically true (pre-authorization).
Authorised claims must use the registered verbatim wording and meet nutrient conditions (e.g. 'source of' requires at least 15% NRV). Free wording like 'boost your immune system' is not authorised.
A permitted health claim must carry the Article 10(2) statements: importance of a varied, balanced diet and healthy lifestyle, and the quantity and pattern of consumption needed for the effect.
No claim may state or imply prevention, treatment, or cure of disease; reduction-of-disease-risk claims need Article 14 authorisation plus the multiple-risk-factors statement.
Practices must not mislead by action or by omission of material information the average consumer needs to make an informed choice (UCPD).
Any data-capture call to action needs freely given, specific, informed, unambiguous opt-in consent (no pre-ticked boxes).
